latest.title: Admission of shares
latest.content:
14 Apr 2026 09:00
RNS Number : 2826A
Amigo Resources PLC
14 April 2026
NOT FOR RELEASE, PUBLICATION OR DISTRIBUTION, IN WHOLE OR IN PART, IN, INTO OR FROM ANY JURISDICTION WHERE TO DO SO WOULD CONSTITUTE A VIOLATION OF THE RELEVANT LAWS OF SUCH JURISDICTION
THIS ANNOUNCEMENT CONTAINS INSIDE INFORMATION
FOR IMMEDIATE RELEASE
14
April 2026
Amigo Resources PLC
("
Amigo
" or the "
Company
")
Admission of shares
Amigo (LSE:AMGO) announced on 7 April 2026 that that it intended to convert Tranche 2 of the Loan Notes (being £375,000 of the £1,500,000 of the Loan Notes as detailed in the Notice of General Meeting published on 26 November 2025 ("
Notice
")) into 125,000,000 new ordinary shares of 0.25p each in the Company ("
New
Ordinary Shares
") on or about 14 April 2026.
The New Ordinary Shares were admitted to listing on the Equity Shares (Commercial Companies) Category of the Official List and to trading on the main market for listed securities of the London Stock Exchange effective at 8.00 am today.
The New Ordinary Shares rank
pari passu
in all respects with the existing ordinary shares of the Company with ISIN GB00BFFK8T45.
Total Voting Rights
Following Admission, the total number of ordinary shares in the Company with voting rights is 1,190,088,160 and this figure may be used by shareholders in the Company as the denominator for the calculation by which they will determine if they are required to notify their interest in, or a change to their interest in, the share capital of the Company under the FCA's Disclosure Guidance and Transparency Rules.
T
his announcement contains inside information for the purposes of Article 7 of the Market Abuse Regulation (EU) 596/2014.
The person responsible for this announcement is Nicholas Beal, Chief Executive.
Amigo Resources PLC
investors@amigo.me
Craig Ransley
Executive Chair
Nick Beal
Chief Executive
Sponsor
Beaumont Cornish
0207 628 3396
About Amigo Resources PLC
Amigo is a public limited company registered in England and Wales with registered number 10024479. Amigo is focussed on gold and rare earth mining opportunities in Africa, principally in Tanzania and Mauritania. The Amigo Shares are listed on the Official List of the London Stock Exchange
Additional Information
This announcement is not intended to, and does not, constitute or form part of any offer, invitation, or the solicitation of an offer to purchase, otherwise acquire, subscribe for, sell, or otherwise dispose of, any securities, or the solicitation of any vote or approval in any jurisdiction, pursuant to this announcement or otherwise.
-ENDS-
This information is provided by RNS, the news service of the London Stock Exchange. RNS is approved by the Financial Conduct Authority to act as a Primary Information Provider in the United Kingdom. Terms and conditions relating to the use and distribution of this information may apply. For further information, please contact

latest.title: Director and PDMR dealing
latest.content:
14 Apr 2026 09:30
RNS Number : 2827A
Amigo Resources PLC
14 April 2026
NOT FOR RELEASE, PUBLICATION OR DISTRIBUTION, IN WHOLE OR IN PART, IN, INTO OR FROM ANY JURISDICTION WHERE TO DO SO WOULD CONSTITUTE A VIOLATION OF THE RELEVANT LAWS OF SUCH JURISDICTION
FOR IMMEDIATE RELEASE
14 April 2026
Amigo Resources PLC
("
Amigo
" "
PLC
" or the "
Company
")
Director and PDMR dealing
On 6 January 2026 Amigo announced (the "
Announcement
") that it had received notification that Nicholas Beal, the CEO, had purchased an interest in ordinary shares of 0.25p each ("
Ordinary Shares
") in Amigo through the purchase of mandatory convertible loan notes ("
Loan Notes
") (as set out in more detail in the Notice of General Meeting published on 26 November 2025 ("
Notice
")) from one of the original subscribers to the Loan Notes.
Tranche 2 of the Loan Notes have converted into Ordinary Shares. As a result (as detailed in the Announcement) Nicholas Beal now holds an additional 916,666 Ordinary Shares in Amigo. Nicholas now holds 9,081,979 Ordinary Shares in Amigo.
Qam Jaffri, now a Non-Executive Director, was one of the original subscribers to the Loan Notes.
As a result of the conversion of Tranche 2 of the Loan Notes, Qam now holds an additional 12,500,000 Ordinary Shares in Amigo. Qam now holds 50,000,000 Ordinary Shares in Amigo.

company_30s.what_they_do: The public filings do not disclose a clear business model. The company appears to be a small, low‑profile entity listed on the London market with no readily available product or service description.
company_30s.why_they_matter: Because it is a listed entity, it could potentially become a target for acquisition, a shell for a reverse takeover, or a vehicle for a new venture. Its presence on the exchange means it is subject to regulatory oversight, but beyond that there is no known market relevance.
company_30s.current_state: There are no recent disclosures, no revenue reports, and no evidence of active operations. The company’s public profile is essentially dormant, trading at a price that reflects its lack of visible fundamentals.

rns_narrative: The company has not released any news items in the past 10 RNS windows, indicating a lack of corporate activity.
There is no evidence of new funding rounds, product launches, or strategic partnerships.
The absence of any public filings suggests the company is either in a hold‑mode or operating under a very limited business scope.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Complete silence – no chatter at all.
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: None. The market has not voiced any optimism.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: None. The market has not voiced any pessimism because there is nothing to react to.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: No movement – the sentiment has remained flat, reflecting the absence of new information.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends data is inactive, confirming that the public has not shown any interest in this ticker.

near_term_catalysts: A regulatory filing (e.g., annual report, financial statements) could provide the first concrete data on the company’s operations.
A corporate action such as a merger, acquisition, or reverse takeover announcement would immediately generate market interest.
Any social media mention by a senior executive or a partner could spark a brief spike in volume and price.
expect_next.watch_for: Look for the first official filing after a prolonged silence, a sudden uptick in trading volume, or a press release announcing a new partnership or product.
expect_next.timing_regime: Without any historical price moves or technical patterns, it is impossible to determine where the stock sits in a crash‑recovery cycle. The only viable assumption is that the stock is in a dormant state awaiting a catalyst.

buy_vs_sell.lean: Neutral – the data does not support a clear contrarian case.
buy_vs_sell.bull_case: There is no evidence of a deep crash, accumulation zone, or any of the signal colors that the system uses to gauge potential upside. Without a defined bottom, a mean‑reversion thesis cannot be justified.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: The company’s lack of public information and absence of any operational or financial data mean that the investment could be a total loss. There is no visible revenue stream, no disclosed assets, and no regulatory filings to validate its existence.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Potential loss of the entire position is realistic; no upside can be calculated because the price is essentially a reflection of the lack of information.

playbook.if_bullish: Not recommended. The absence of any concrete data makes a bullish position untenable.
playbook.if_watching: Hold a small position (if any) and monitor for the first official filing or press release. A sudden increase in volume or a new announcement would be the only trigger for a considered entry.
playbook.if_passing: If the company fails to file any regulatory documents within the next 3–6 months or if it is delisted, exit immediately to preserve capital.

ai_insights.bottom_line: Without any visible fundamentals or a clear crash point, this stock offers no actionable contrarian upside; the risk of total loss outweighs any potential reward.
